The CAIDA dataset is a specific instance of the broader internet traffic dataset used for networking experiments.
The network data is collected at a Tier1 ISP's data center located in New York City.
Each minute of IP data contains approximately 3.5 million packets.
Chunks are created by splitting 7 minutes of data into smaller units of approximately 170 milliseconds.
Each chunk consists of approximately 105 packets.
Distributions are derived from the empirical distribution of source IP addresses in each data chunk.
The ultimate objective of the experiments is to perform density estimation using the collected network data.
